page.primary_keyword: F1 Grand Prix 2028 Marbella yacht charter
page.title: F1 Grand Prix 2028: Marbella Yacht Charter for Race Weekend

data.meta_description: Book an F1 Grand Prix 2028 Marbella yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Watch the race from the sea with our fleet—€749/2h includes skipper, drinks & VAT.
data.summary: Experience the F1 Grand Prix 2028 from the Mediterranean with a Marbella yacht charter. Depart from Puerto Banús, watch the race on board, and enjoy skipper, drinks, and snacks included.
key_facts: F1 Grand Prix 2028: 2–4 June 2028 (tbc) at Circuito de Jerez, 90 NM from Marbella.
Charter from Puerto Banús: Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 from €749/2h, all inclusive.
All boats include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (beer, wine, cava, soft drinks), snacks, insurance & VAT.
Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) available from €4,719/4h with a jet ski included free.
Watch the race on board with our onboard TV screens and sound systems.
Anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés between practice sessions; swim stops included.
Book via WhatsApp for custom durations, catering upgrades, or multi-day packages.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús; private transfers from Marbella hotels available.

faq.q: Can I watch the F1 Grand Prix live from a yacht in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes. All our charter boats are equipped with TV screens and sound systems to stream the race live. You can anchor off the coast of Marbella or Puerto Banús and enjoy the race with drinks and snacks on deck.

faq.q: How far is the F1 circuit from Marbella?
faq.a: The Circuito de Jerez is about 90 nautical miles (166 km) west of Marbella. A direct cruise takes 2–3 hours on a motor yacht. We recommend an 8-hour charter (€2,299) to combine the crossing with swimming stops and lunch.

faq.q: What boats are best for a group of 10–12 guests?
faq.a: For up to 12 guests, the Mangusta 80 (24m, €4,719/4h with jet ski) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) are ideal. The Ferretti 94 (29m) offers extra luxury. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: Do you offer F1-themed catering or packages?
faq.a: We can arrange upgraded catering (champagne, seafood platters, paella) on request via WhatsApp. Standard charters already include beer, wine, cava, soft drinks, water, and light snacks.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht for the whole F1 weekend?
faq.a: Yes. Multi-day charters are available for the F1 weekend (Friday practice, Saturday qualifying, Sunday race). Contact us via WhatsApp for pricing and availability. Overnight stays possible on larger yachts like the Ferretti 94.

faq.q: What is included in the F1 Grand Prix 2028 yacht charter price?
faq.a: All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

faq.q: Where do we depart from for the F1 charter?
faq.a: All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. We can arrange a private transfer from your hotel or villa on the Golden Mile or Marbella town centre.

faq.q: What if the weather is bad on race day?
faq.a: Our skippers monitor conditions closely. If the forecast is unsafe, we reschedule or offer a full refund. Marbella averages 300 sunny days a year, so weather disruptions are rare.

data.body_html:
The F1 Grand Prix 2028 promises another thrilling weekend of speed, glamour, and Mediterranean sunshine. A Marbella yacht charter offers the most exclusive way to experience the race—anchor off the coast of Puerto Banús, watch the action on board, and enjoy the luxury of a private boat with skipper, drinks, and snacks included.

F1 Grand Prix 2028: Dates and Circuit

The 2028 Spanish Grand Prix is expected to take place from 2–4 June 2028 (dates subject to FIA confirmation) at the Circuito de Jerez – Ángel Nieto, approximately 90 nautical miles west of Marbella. The 4.4 km track hosts practice on Friday, qualifying on Saturday, and the main race on Sunday. With our yacht charter, you can cruise to Jerez or watch the race live from the comfort of your boat in Marbella.

Why Choose a Yacht Charter for the F1 Weekend?

Forget crowded grandstands and overpriced paddock clubs. A private charter gives you:

  • Uninterrupted views of the race on onboard TV screens
  • Swim stops at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés between sessions
  • All-inclusive drinks and snacks—no bar queues
  • Flexible timing: 2-hour, 4-hour, or full-day charters
  • Departure from Puerto Banús, the epicentre of Marbella luxury

Our Fleet for the F1 Grand Prix 2028

We operate a range of motor yachts and speedboats from Puerto Banús. For the F1 weekend, we recommend:
Tier A (€749/2h → €2,299/8h): Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax). Ideal for small groups. Includes skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Tier B (€4,719/4h minimum): Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) in white or grey. Includes a free jet ski. Perfect for entertaining clients or a large group.
Other boats: Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax), Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax)—pricing on request via WhatsApp.

Sample F1 Weekend Itinerary

Friday (Practice): 4-hour charter (€1,499 Tier A). Depart Puerto Banús at 10:00. Cruise south to Cabopino, anchor for a swim. Watch FP1 and FP2 on board. Return at 14:00.
Saturday (Qualifying): 6-hour charter (€1,899 Tier A). Depart 13:00, head to Cala Cortés for lunch and swimming. Watch qualifying at 15:00. Return at 19:00.
Sunday (Race): 8-hour charter (€2,299 Tier A). Depart 11:00. Cruise to Estepona for a swim stop. Watch the race live at 15:00. Return to Puerto Banús at 19:00 for post-race celebrations.

What’s Included in Every Charter

All our charters include: licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees. For upgraded catering—champagne, seafood platters, paella—contact us via WhatsApp.

How to Book Your F1 Grand Prix 2028 Yacht Charter

Reserve your preferred boat early—F1 weekend is peak season. Visit our Boat Rental Marbella homepage to browse the full fleet, then message us on WhatsApp with your preferred boat, date, and duration. We’ll confirm availability and send a secure payment link. All charters depart from Puerto Banús; we can arrange transfers from your Marbella hotel.

Frequently asked questions

Can I watch the F1 Grand Prix live from a yacht in Marbella?

Yes. All our charter boats are equipped with TV screens and sound systems to stream the race live. You can anchor off the coast of Marbella or Puerto Banús and enjoy the race with drinks and snacks on deck.

How far is the F1 circuit from Marbella?

The Circuito de Jerez is about 90 nautical miles (166 km) west of Marbella. A direct cruise takes 2–3 hours on a motor yacht. We recommend an 8-hour charter (€2,299) to combine the crossing with swimming stops and lunch.

What boats are best for a group of 10–12 guests?

For up to 12 guests, the Mangusta 80 (24m, €4,719/4h with jet ski) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) are ideal. The Ferretti 94 (29m) offers extra luxury. All depart from Puerto Banús.

Do you offer F1-themed catering or packages?

We can arrange upgraded catering (champagne, seafood platters, paella) on request via WhatsApp. Standard charters already include beer, wine, cava, soft drinks, water, and light snacks.

Can I charter a yacht for the whole F1 weekend?

Yes. Multi-day charters are available for the F1 weekend (Friday practice, Saturday qualifying, Sunday race). Contact us via WhatsApp for pricing and availability. Overnight stays possible on larger yachts like the Ferretti 94.

What is included in the F1 Grand Prix 2028 yacht charter price?

All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras.

Where do we depart from for the F1 charter?

All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. We can arrange a private transfer from your hotel or villa on the Golden Mile or Marbella town centre.

What if the weather is bad on race day?

Our skippers monitor conditions closely. If the forecast is unsafe, we reschedule or offer a full refund. Marbella averages 300 sunny days a year, so weather disruptions are rare.
